prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. The land, air, and sea blockade of the Gaza Strip was imposed by Israel and Egypt temporarily in 2005-2006 and permanently from 2007 onwards, following the Israeli disengagement from Gaza. This temporary blockade was made permanent after Hamas took over government institutions and replaced PA officials with Hamas members. Israel and the Quartet on the Middle East established conditions before the PA continued to provide aid or dialogue with any member of a Hamas-led PA government, including: recognition of Israel, abandonment of violent acts, and unconditionally.
B. The Gaza Strip is currently experiencing a land, air, and sea blockade, which has been continuously enforced by both Israel and Egypt. This blockade was initially implemented on a temporary basis between 2005 and 2006, becoming a permanent measure starting in 2007. Its establishment followed Israel's withdrawal from Gaza. The decision to make the blockade permanent occurred after Hamas gained control of the Gaza Strip during the conflict known as the Battle of Gaza. During this event, Hamas seized control of governmental bodies and replaced officials affiliated with Fatah and other branches of the Palestinian Authority (PA) with its own members. Prior to this, in March 2006, after Hamas had emerged victorious in elections and subsequently established the PA government under the leadership of Ismail Haniya, Israel, along with the Quartet on the Middle East, stipulated certain conditions. These conditions had to be met before they would continue offering assistance to the PA or engage in any discussions with individuals who were part of a Hamas-led PA government. The specified conditions included acknowledging the state of Israel and renouncing any acts of violence.
C. The blockade of the Gaza Strip is the ongoing land, air, and sea blockade of the Gaza Strip imposed by Israel and Egypt temporarily in 2005–2006 and permanently from 2007 onwards, following the Israeli disengagement from Gaza.The blockade was made permanent after Hamas took control of the Gaza Strip during the Battle of Gaza, seizing government institutions and replacing Fatah and other Palestinian Authority (PA) officials with Hamas members. Earlier, after Hamas won elections and formed the PA government in March 2006, led by Ismail Haniya, Israel and the Quartet on the Middle East set conditions before they would continue to provide aid to the PA or have any dialogue with any member of a Hamas-led PA government. These conditions were: recognition of Israel, disavowal of violent actions,

Answer:

This temporary blockade was made permanent after Hamas took over government institutions and replaced PA officials with Hamas members. Israel and the Quartet on the Middle East established conditions before the PA continued to provide aid or dialogue with any member of a Hamas-led PA government, including: recognition of Israel, abandonment of violent acts, and unconditionally.
options.B: The Gaza Strip is currently experiencing a land, air, and sea blockade, which has been continuously enforced by both Israel and Egypt. This blockade was initially implemented on a temporary basis between 2005 and 2006, becoming a permanent measure starting in 2007. Its establishment followed Israel's withdrawal from Gaza. The decision to make the blockade permanent occurred after Hamas gained control of the Gaza Strip during the conflict known as the Battle of Gaza. During this event, Hamas seized control of governmental bodies and replaced officials affiliated with Fatah and other branches of the Palestinian Authority (PA) with its own members. Prior to this, in March 2006, after Hamas had emerged victorious in elections and subsequently established the PA government under the leadership of Ismail Haniya, Israel, along with the Quartet on the Middle East, stipulated certain conditions. These conditions had to be met before they would continue offering assistance to the PA or engage in any discussions with individuals who were part of a Hamas-led PA government. The specified conditions included acknowledging the state of Israel and renouncing any acts of violence.
